scope: Actual LightningIndexer layer-2 compressed dKV backward on 1xMI350, focused on why prior write-coalescing probes failed and what the MFMA-shaped replacement must preserve.

retained_fast_contribution_path.name: dot_k_atomic
retained_fast_contribution_path.read: This branch keeps the fast tl.dot KxD contribution body, but writes one global atomic row per valid selected compressed group. It is the current sparse-atomic wall to beat.

dotk_partial_store.read: This computes each KxD contribution once, but stores the raw contribution volume into a global partial slab before reducing. Removing final atomics without reducing in on-chip storage does not beat dot_k_atomic.

dotk_micro_active_groups.read: Reduces global writes, but recomputes the KxD contribution for each active group block.
Wider selected block improves write reduction, but repeated contribution generation still dominates.
This approaches the first write-reduction rung, but the larger route matrix and repeated contribution body make it much slower.

scalar_dense_group_hip.read: The local compressed-group table is correct and collapses writes, but the body serializes the head reduction and uses scalar LDS atomics. It fails before the S4096 gate.

mechanism_read.why_previous_branches_fail: The winning contribution body and the winning write-count body have not yet been combined. dot_k_atomic computes contribution once but writes too much; dot_k_micro and fixed-panel variants reduce writes but recompute contribution for each active group block or group panel; scalar HIP local-table reduces writes but loses MFMA/tl.dot head reduction.
mechanism_read.required_next_kernel_shape: Compute contrib_kd = grad_logits^T * q * scale + probs^T * dO once per query tile, selected-key tile, head tile, and D16 block using MFMA/tensor-core style math; then accumulate selected rows into a tile-local compressed-group table or hash/segmented reducer before global writes.
must_not_do: Do not materialize dense [H,S,G] route tensors.
Do not write a global partial slab with the raw selected KxD contribution volume.
Do not recompute contrib_kd once per active group block or fixed group panel.
Do not fall back to scalar per-head reductions in the hot body.

rocwmma_compile_smoke.read: A minimal rocWMMA 16x16x16 half->float mma_sync kernel compiles in the current image. This only proves toolchain viability; it is not an implemented dKV kernel.

next_executable_rung.name: rocwmma_or_cktile_dotk_contrib_tile_smoke
next_executable_rung.goal: Prove a HIP extension can compute the KxD contribution tile with MFMA-style math and match the existing Triton dot_k/PyTorch contribution oracle before adding local group accumulation.
next_executable_rung.inputs: Materialized diagnostic q, grad_output, probs, grad_logits, safe_groups, valid, with seq_chunk=32, selected_block initially 64 or 128, block_h=64, block_d=16.
next_executable_rung.first_gate: S128/S512 contribution-tile parity and compile on gfx950.
next_executable_rung.promotion_gate: After adding tile-local grouped accumulation, S512 must beat 3.08 ms and S4096 must beat the 8.6-8.8 ms sparse-atomic wall while avoiding selected-K/V, probability, global partial, or dense-route slabs.
